title: FEL Function Catalog
description: Structured catalog of all built-in functions in the Formspec Expression Language (FEL) v1.0. Each entry defines the function's name, signature, return type, null handling, and usage examples. This catalog is the normative reference for FEL function behavior; implementations in TypeScript (packages/formspec-engine) and Python (src/formspec/fel) must conform to these signatures and semantics.

$formspecFelFunctions.description: FEL function catalog specification version. MUST be '1.0'.

x-lm.intent: Version pin for FEL function catalog document compatibility.

FELType.description: A FEL type identifier. 'any' means the function accepts or returns multiple types. 'array' means array<T> where T is specified in the description.

enum.description: When present, restricts the parameter to these literal values.

name.description: Function name as used in FEL expressions.

category.description: Functional category for grouping and documentation.

parameters.description: Ordered parameter list. Variadic parameters must be last.

returnDescription.description: Clarification of the return value when 'returns' alone is insufficient.

description.description: What the function does — behavior, edge cases, and constraints.

nullHandling.description: How the function behaves when one or more arguments are null.

deterministic.description: False if the function can return different results for the same arguments (e.g., today, now).

shortCircuit.description: True if the function evaluates arguments lazily (e.g., if only evaluates the selected branch).

x-generated-from: fel-core (https://github.com/Formspec-org/fel-core) — regenerate via `cargo run -p fel-core --bin emit-fel-schema > formspec/schemas/fel-functions.schema.json`

functions.name: sum

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: Array of numbers (or money objects — extracts .amount).

functions.description: Sums all numeric elements in the array. Extracts .amount from money objects. Non-finite values treated as 0.
functions.nullHandling: Null elements are skipped. Null argument returns 0.

functions.name: count
functions.description: Returns the number of elements in the array, including nulls.
functions.nullHandling: Null argument returns 0. Null elements ARE counted.

functions.name: countWhere

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.description: FEL expression evaluated per element with '$' rebound to the current element.

functions.description: Counts array elements for which the predicate evaluates to true. The predicate receives each element via '$' (the self-reference is rebound per element). Special argument handling: the predicate is NOT pre-evaluated — it is evaluated once per element.
functions.nullHandling: Null array returns 0. Null predicate result counts as false.

functions.name: sumWhere

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: Array of numbers (or elements from which numeric matches are taken).

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.description: FEL expression evaluated per element with '$' rebound to the current element.

functions.description: Sums numeric array elements for which the predicate evaluates to true. The predicate is NOT pre-evaluated — it is evaluated once per element.
functions.nullHandling: Null array returns null. Non-numeric matches are skipped. No numeric matches returns 0.

functions.name: avgWhere

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.description: FEL expression evaluated per element with '$' rebound to the current element.

functions.description: Arithmetic mean of numeric elements for which the predicate is true. Returns null when no elements match.
functions.nullHandling: Null array returns null. No matches returns null.

functions.name: minWhere

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.description: FEL expression evaluated per element with '$' rebound to the current element.

functions.description: Smallest element among those satisfying the predicate. Also applies to dates and strings.
functions.nullHandling: Null array returns null. No matches returns null.

functions.name: maxWhere

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.description: FEL expression evaluated per element with '$' rebound to the current element.

functions.description: Largest element among those satisfying the predicate. Also applies to dates and strings.
functions.nullHandling: Null array returns null. No matches returns null.

functions.name: every

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.description: FEL expression evaluated per element with '$' rebound to the current element.

functions.description: True if the array is empty or the predicate is true for every element. The predicate is NOT pre-evaluated — it is evaluated once per element.
functions.nullHandling: Null array returns null. Null predicate result is not true (element fails every, does not satisfy some).

functions.name: some

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.description: FEL expression evaluated per element with '$' rebound to the current element.

functions.description: True if at least one element satisfies the predicate. The predicate is NOT pre-evaluated — it is evaluated once per element.
functions.nullHandling: Null array returns null. Null predicate result counts as false.

functions.name: avg
functions.description: Arithmetic mean of all finite numeric elements. Skips nulls and non-numeric values.
functions.nullHandling: Null/non-numeric elements skipped. Empty array or all-null returns 0.

functions.name: min
functions.description: Returns the smallest finite numeric value in the array.
functions.nullHandling: Null/non-numeric elements skipped. Empty array returns 0.

functions.name: max
functions.description: Returns the largest finite numeric value in the array.
functions.nullHandling: Null/non-numeric elements skipped. Empty array returns 0.

functions.name: length
functions.description: Returns the number of characters in the string.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns 0.

functions.name: contains
functions.description: Returns true if haystack contains needle. Case-sensitive.
functions.nullHandling: Null haystack treated as empty string.

functions.name: startsWith
functions.description: Returns true if value starts with prefix. Case-sensitive.
functions.nullHandling: Null value treated as empty string.

functions.name: endsWith
functions.description: Returns true if value ends with suffix. Case-sensitive.
functions.nullHandling: Null value treated as empty string.

functions.name: substring
functions.description: Extracts a substring starting at the 1-based position. If length is omitted, extracts to the end of the string.
functions.nullHandling: Null value treated as empty string.

functions.name: replace
functions.description: Replaces ALL occurrences of the search literal with the replacement. Not regex — literal string match only.
functions.nullHandling: Null value treated as empty string.

functions.name: upper
functions.description: Converts string to uppercase.
functions.nullHandling: Null treated as empty string, returns empty string.

functions.name: lower
functions.description: Converts string to lowercase.
functions.nullHandling: Null treated as empty string, returns empty string.

functions.name: trim
functions.description: Removes leading and trailing whitespace.
functions.nullHandling: Null treated as empty string, returns empty string.

functions.name: matches
functions.description: Returns true if the string matches the regular expression pattern. Pattern syntax follows the host language's regex engine (ECMA-262 for TypeScript, Python re for Python).
functions.nullHandling: Null value treated as empty string.

functions.name: format
functions.description: Positional string interpolation. Replaces {0}, {1}, etc. in the template with stringified arguments. Null arguments become empty string. Numbers strip trailing zeros. Booleans become 'true'/'false'.
functions.nullHandling: Null template returns empty string. Null arguments substituted as empty string.

functions.name: round
functions.description: Rounds to the specified number of decimal places using banker's rounding (round half to even).
functions.nullHandling: Null treated as 0.

examples.note: Banker's rounding: half rounds to even

functions.name: floor
functions.description: Returns the largest integer less than or equal to the value.
functions.nullHandling: Null treated as 0.

functions.name: ceil
functions.description: Returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to the value.
functions.nullHandling: Null treated as 0.

functions.name: abs

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Number to take absolute value of.

functions.description: Returns the absolute value of the number.
functions.nullHandling: Null treated as 0.

functions.name: power
functions.description: Returns base raised to the power of exponent.
functions.nullHandling: Null arguments treated as 0.

functions.name: today
functions.description: Returns the current date as an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD). Non-deterministic.
functions.nullHandling: N/A — no parameters.

functions.name: now
functions.description: Returns the current date and time as an ISO 8601 dateTime string. Non-deterministic.
functions.nullHandling: N/A — no parameters.

functions.name: year
functions.description: Extracts the 4-digit year from a date.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns null.

functions.name: month
functions.description: Extracts the month (1-12) from a date.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns null.

functions.name: day
functions.description: Extracts the day of month (1-31) from a date.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns null.

functions.name: hours
functions.description: Extracts the hour component (0-23) from a dateTime or time value.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns null.

functions.name: minutes
functions.description: Extracts the minute component (0-59) from a dateTime or time value.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns null.

functions.name: seconds
functions.description: Extracts the second component (0-59) from a dateTime or time value.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns null.

functions.name: time
functions.description: Constructs an HH:MM:SS time string from numeric components.
functions.nullHandling: Null components treated as 0.

functions.name: dateDiff
functions.description: Returns the difference date1 - date2 in the specified unit. Result is positive when date1 > date2, negative when date1 < date2. For months/years, incomplete periods are truncated (not rounded).
functions.nullHandling: Null or invalid dates return null.

functions.name: dateAdd

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Number of units to add (negative to subtract).

functions.description: Adds the specified number of units to a date. Negative values subtract. Month/year arithmetic handles end-of-month overflow per the host language's Date implementation.
functions.nullHandling: Null date returns null.

functions.name: timeDiff
functions.returnDescription: Signed difference in whole seconds: laterTime minus earlierTime (positive when the first argument is later in the day).
functions.description: Difference in seconds between two time-of-day strings. Distinct from duration(), which parses an ISO 8601 duration and returns milliseconds.
functions.nullHandling: Invalid time strings produce a diagnostic and null.

functions.name: duration

parameters.name: isoDuration
parameters.description: ISO 8601 duration (PnYnMnDTnHnMnS subset), optional leading minus.

functions.description: Parses a duration string and returns its length in milliseconds. Years/months in the date part use fixed 365-day years and 30-day months (not calendar arithmetic). Distinct from timeDiff, which compares two clock times in seconds.
functions.nullHandling: Null argument returns null. Invalid strings produce an error diagnostic and null.

functions.name: if

parameters.name: thenValue
parameters.description: Value returned when condition is true.

parameters.name: elseValue
parameters.description: Value returned when condition is false.

functions.returnDescription: Type matches whichever branch is selected.
functions.description: Conditional function. Returns thenValue when condition is true, elseValue when false. Only the selected branch is evaluated (short-circuit). Alternative syntax: 'if cond then a else b' (keyword form). Note: 'if' is a reserved word; this function is special-cased in the parser.
functions.nullHandling: Null condition is an evaluation error.

functions.name: coalesce
functions.description: Returns the first argument that is not null, undefined, or empty string. If all arguments are null/empty, returns null.
functions.nullHandling: Core purpose is null handling — returns first non-null, non-empty value.

functions.name: empty
functions.description: Returns true if the value is null, undefined, empty string (''), or an empty array ([]). Broader than a simple null check.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns true (that's the point).

functions.name: present
functions.description: Inverse of empty(). Returns true if the value is non-null, non-empty-string, and non-empty-array.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns false.

functions.name: selected

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Field value (string for choice, array for multiChoice).

functions.description: For multiChoice (array): returns true if the option is included in the array. For choice (string): returns true if value equals option. Designed for testing selected options in choice/multiChoice fields.
functions.nullHandling: Null value returns false.

functions.name: isNumber
functions.description: Returns true if the value is a finite number (not NaN).
functions.nullHandling: Null returns false.

functions.name: isString
functions.description: Returns true if the value is a string.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns false.

functions.name: isDate
functions.description: Returns true if the value can be parsed as a valid date.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns false.

functions.name: isNull
functions.description: Returns true if the value is null, undefined, or empty string. Note: broader than a strict null check — empty string is also considered 'null' in FEL.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns true (that's the point).

functions.name: typeOf
functions.description: Returns the FEL type name of the value.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns 'null'.

functions.name: number
functions.description: Explicit type cast to number. Strings are parsed as numbers. Returns null if coercion fails.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns null.

functions.name: string
functions.description: Explicit type cast to string. Null becomes empty string. Numbers, booleans, and dates are stringified.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns empty string ''.

functions.name: boolean
functions.description: Explicit type cast to boolean. Accepts: booleans (pass-through), numbers (0 = false, non-zero = true), strings 'true'/'false'. Other values produce an evaluation error.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns false.

functions.name: date

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to validate/coerce as ISO 8601 date.

functions.description: Validates and returns the input as an ISO 8601 date string. If the input is not a valid date, produces an evaluation error.
functions.nullHandling: Null returns null.

functions.name: money
functions.description: Constructs a money object from an amount and currency code.
functions.nullHandling: Null arguments produce a money object with null/undefined fields.

functions.name: moneyAmount
functions.description: Extracts the numeric amount from a money object.
functions.nullHandling: Null or non-money value returns null.

functions.name: moneyCurrency
functions.description: Extracts the currency code from a money object.
functions.nullHandling: Null or non-money value returns null.

functions.name: moneyAdd

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Second money object. Must have the same currency as first.

functions.description: Adds two money objects. Uses the currency from the first non-null operand. Per the spec, both operands SHOULD have the same currency; implementations MAY error on mismatched currencies.
functions.nullHandling: Null operand returns null.

functions.name: moneySum
functions.description: Sums an array of money objects. Returns a money object with the currency from the first element. All elements SHOULD have the same currency.
functions.nullHandling: Null elements skipped. Empty array returns null.

functions.name: moneySumWhere

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.description: FEL expression evaluated per element with '$' rebound to the current money value.

functions.description: Sums money array elements for which the predicate evaluates to true. Matched elements MUST share the same currency. The predicate is NOT pre-evaluated — it is evaluated once per element.
functions.nullHandling: Null array returns null. No matches returns null.

functions.name: locale
functions.description: Returns the active BCP 47 locale tag from the evaluation context.
functions.nullHandling: When the host has not set a locale, returns null.

functions.name: runtimeMeta

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Key in the host-provided runtime metadata bag.

functions.description: Reads a value from runtime metadata supplied by the host.
functions.nullHandling: Missing key returns null.

functions.name: pluralCategory

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Numeric count for plural rule selection.

parameters.name: localeTag
parameters.description: Optional BCP 47 tag; defaults to locale().

functions.description: Returns the CLDR cardinal plural category (zero, one, two, few, many, other) for the count and locale.
functions.nullHandling: Null count returns null.

functions.name: valid

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Field path (NOT evaluated as expression — extracted as literal path string).

functions.description: Returns true if the field at the given path has zero validation errors. The argument is a field reference path, not a general expression — the parser extracts it as a literal string rather than evaluating it.
functions.nullHandling: N/A — path is a literal reference.

functions.name: relevant
functions.description: Returns the computed relevance (visibility) state of the field at the given path. True means the field is visible/active.
functions.nullHandling: N/A — path is a literal reference.

functions.name: readonly
functions.description: Returns the computed readonly state of the field at the given path.
functions.nullHandling: N/A — path is a literal reference.

functions.name: required
functions.description: Returns the computed required state of the field at the given path.
functions.nullHandling: N/A — path is a literal reference.

functions.name: prev

parameters.name: fieldName
parameters.description: Name of the sibling field to read from the previous repeat instance.

functions.description: Returns the value of the named field from the previous repeat instance (index - 1). Must be called within a repeat context. Returns null if at the first instance or not inside a repeat.
functions.nullHandling: Returns null when no previous instance exists.

functions.name: next

parameters.name: fieldName
parameters.description: Name of the sibling field to read from the next repeat instance.

functions.description: Returns the value of the named field from the next repeat instance (index + 1). Must be called within a repeat context. Returns null if at the last instance or not inside a repeat.
functions.nullHandling: Returns null when no next instance exists.

functions.name: parent

parameters.name: fieldName
parameters.description: Name of the ancestor field to find.

functions.description: Walks up the path hierarchy from the current item and returns the value of the first ancestor field matching the given name. Useful for accessing enclosing group data from within nested repeats.
functions.nullHandling: Returns null if no ancestor field with that name is found.

functions.name: instance

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the secondary data source (must match a key in the definition's 'instances' object).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Dot-notation path within the instance data.

functions.description: Retrieves data from a named secondary instance. Typically invoked via the '@instance("name")' context reference syntax in FEL, which the parser translates to this function call. The optional path parameter drills into the instance data.
functions.nullHandling: Returns null/undefined if instance name not found or path doesn't exist.
